item.title: Spine Check: Talking tough, voting soft
item.description: A Spine Check example showing the gap between rhetoric and recorded action.

item.title: Receipts: “We never supported that”
item.description: A receipts-first example showing how public denial collapses under primary sources.

item.title: Receipts: Trump at a Ford plant — the heckler, the f-bomb, and the finger
item.description: Receipts-first: Trump’s Ford plant visit in Dearborn and the moment captured on video.

item.title: Welcome to cunexttuesday.org
item.description: First post: why this blog is plain HTML/CSS on Cloudflare Pages.

item.title: Receipts template example
item.description: Example layout showing quotes, timeline, and sources in the Receipts format.

item.title: Receipts: The Minneapolis ICE shooting of Renee Nicole Good
item.description: Receipts-first: what video, eyewitness reporting, and official statements say about the ICE shooting of Renee Nicole Good in Minneapolis.

item.title: ‘Privately concerned’ → publicly compliant: the anonymous GOP pattern
item.description: A meta-entry: the recurring ‘private concern’ leak that never becomes a vote, a statement, or a break.

item.title: DOJ independence: what it is, why it matters, and what to watch
item.description: A receipts-first explainer on DOJ independence concerns in a second Trump administration, and the kinds of actions that raise red flags.

item.title: Private doubts → public amplification: Fox, Dominion, and the record
item.description: Discovery and reporting described internal doubts even as false claims aired; Dominion settled for $787.5M.

item.title: Ending TPS for Somalis: what the decision does, and what’s on the record
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to terminate Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for Somalis, with the stated rationale and the timeline for protections ending.

item.title: ‘We won’t cave’ → then: deadlines, stopgaps, and retreat (Freedom Caucus)
item.description: A rolling entry tracking hardline demands vs endgame deals around shutdown/debt fights.

item.title: Immigration: what changed since Jan 20, 2025 (paper trail edition)
item.description: A receipts-first map of Trump administration immigration executive actions since Jan 20, 2025 — using official summaries and primary sources.

item.title: Working-class branding → party-line outcomes: Hawley’s populism vs record
item.description: Hawley markets as working-class populist; the ledger tracks policy outcomes and reversals.

item.title: Environment: EPA’s Clean Water Act §401 memo and what it signals
item.description: EPA issued a May 21, 2025 memorandum clarifying limits on states’ and Tribes’ roles under Clean Water Act Section 401 certification — with implications for project permitting fights.

item.title: ‘Follow the Constitution’ → then: led effort to overturn 2020 results
item.description: Johnson invokes constitutional language while having backed efforts challenging 2020 results.

item.title: Environment/rulemaking: EPA proposes narrowing states’ and Tribes’ Clean Water Act §401 leverage
item.description: AP and Reuters report the Trump administration proposed revising a Section 401 rule to limit states’ and Tribes’ ability to block or condition projects on water-quality grounds.

item.title: ‘No right to overturn’ → then: the movement turns on Pence
item.description: Pence said he had no right to overturn the election; that refusal became a defining rupture.

item.title: Environment: EPA says it will stop calculating health savings from key air pollution rules
item.description: AP reports EPA will stop calculating monetized health benefits (health care savings/lives saved) for certain air pollution rules, focusing on business costs instead.

item.title: ‘Bears responsibility’ → walk-back: McCarthy on Jan 6
item.description: McCarthy said Trump 'bears responsibility' for Jan 6 — then the party line reasserted itself.

item.title: Immigration leverage fight: DOJ drops appeal on tying transportation funds to enforcement cooperation
item.description: A federal court blocked a Trump administration plan to condition transportation funds on immigration enforcement cooperation; DOJ later dropped its appeal.
